question: Why do coding agents struggle on long-horizon tasks?

context.abstract: This fixture is a local domain demo, not the main QASPER experiment.

sections.text: Coding agents can drift over long tasks when intermediate assumptions are not checked, context grows stale, and tool errors are ignored.

reference_answer: Coding agents struggle because planning drift, stale context, and unchecked tool errors compound over long tasks.

evidence.text: Coding agents can drift over long tasks when intermediate assumptions are not checked, context grows stale, and tool errors are ignored.
